title: Shaded areas
description: Find the areas of shaded regions which are combinations of rectangles and circles.
topic_path: Topic D: Problems involving area and surface area

assessment_items.question_content:
A circle with radius of $\greenD{4\,\text{cm}}$ sits inside a circle with radius of $\blueD{11\,\text{cm}}$. 

 ** What is the area of the shaded region? **  
*Round your final answer to the nearest hundredth.* 

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
First, calculate the area of the whole figure, including the unshaded area.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a circle is $\pi r^2$.
 
 $\blueD{\pi \times 11\,\text{cm} \times 11\,\text{cm} = 121\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$

Next, calculate the area of the inner figure.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{\pi \times 4\,\text{cm} \times 4 \,\text{cm}= 16\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$
Finally, subtract the area of the inner circle from the area of the outer circle.
$\blueD{121\pi\,\text{cm}^2} - \greenD{16\pi\,\text{cm}^2} = 105\pi\,\text{cm}^2 \approx 329.87\,\text{cm}^2$

options.alt: A circle with a smaller circle inside it. The circles share a common centre. The radius of the smaller circle is 4 cm and the radius of the bigger circle is 11 cm. The region between both the circles is shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
A circle with radius of $\greenD{3\,\text{cm}}$ sits inside a circle with radius of $\blueD{11\,\text{cm}}$. 

** What is the area of the shaded region? **  
*Round your final answer to the nearest hundredth.* 

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
First, calculate the area of the whole figure, including the unshaded area.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a circle is $\pi r^2$.
 
$\blueD{\pi \times 11\,\text{cm} \times 11\,\text{cm} = 121\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$

Next, calculate the area of the inner figure.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{\pi \times 3\,\text{cm} \times 3 \,\text{cm}= 9\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$
Finally, subtract the area of the inner circle from the area of the outer circle.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{121\pi\,\text{cm}^2} - \greenD{9\pi\,\text{cm}^2} \\\\
&= 112\pi\,\text{cm}^2 \\\\
&\approx 351.86\,\text{cm}^2
\end{align}$

options.alt: A circle with a smaller circle inside it. The circles share a common centre. The radius of the smaller circle is 3 cm and the radius of the bigger circle is 11 cm. The region between both the circles is shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
A circle with radius of $\greenD{9\,\text{cm}}$ sits inside a circle with radius of $\blueD{11\,\text{cm}}$. 

** What is the area of the shaded region? **  
*Round your final answer to the nearest hundredth.* 

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
First, calculate the area of the whole figure, including the unshaded area.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a circle is $\pi r^2$.
 
$\blueD{\pi \times 11\,\text{cm} \times 11\,\text{cm} = 121\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$

Next, calculate the area of the inner figure.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{\pi \times 9\,\text{cm} \times 9 \,\text{cm}= 81\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$
Finally, subtract the area of the inner circle from the area of the outer circle.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{121\pi\,\text{cm}^2} - \greenD{81\pi\,\text{cm}^2} \\\\
&= 40\pi\,\text{cm}^2 \\\\
&\approx 125.66\,\text{cm}^2
\end{align}$

options.alt: A circle with a smaller circle inside it. The circles share a common center. The radius of the smaller circle is 9 cm and the radius of the bigger circle is 11 cm. The region between both the circles is shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
A circle with radius of $\greenD{6\,\text{cm}}$ sits inside a circle with radius of $\blueD{9\,\text{cm}}$. 

** What is the area of the shaded region? **  
*Round your final answer to the nearest hundredth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
First, calculate the area of the whole figure, including the unshaded area.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a circle is $\pi r^2$.
 
$\blueD{\pi \times 9\,\text{cm} \times 9\,\text{cm} = 81\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$

Next, calculate the area of the inner figure.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{\pi \times 6\,\text{cm} \times 6 \,\text{cm}= 36\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$
Finally, subtract the area of the inner circle from the area of the outer circle.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{81\pi\,\text{cm}^2} - \greenD{36\pi\,\text{cm}^2} \\\\
&= 45\pi\,\text{cm}^2 \\\\
&\approx 141.37\,\text{cm}^2
\end{align}$

options.alt: A circle with a smaller circle inside it. The circles share a common centre. The radius of the smaller circle is 6 cm and the radius of the bigger circle is 9 cm. The region between both the circles is shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
A circle with radius of $\greenD{2\,\text{cm}}$ sits inside a circle with radius of $\blueD{4\,\text{cm}}$. 

** What is the area of the shaded region? **  
*Round your final answer to the nearest hundredth.*


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
First, calculate the area of the whole figure, including the unshaded area.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a circle is $\pi r^2$.
 
$\blueD{\pi \times 4\,\text{cm} \times 4\,\text{cm} = 16\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$

Next, calculate the area of the inner figure.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{\pi \times 2\,\text{cm} \times 2 \,\text{cm}= 4\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$
Finally, subtract the area of the inner circle from the area of the outer circle.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{16\pi\,\text{cm}^2} - \greenD{4\pi\,\text{cm}^2} \\\\
&= 12\pi\,\text{cm}^2 \\\\
&\approx 37.7\,\text{cm}^2
\end{align}$

options.alt: A circle with a smaller circle inside it. The circles share a common center. The radius of the smaller circle is 2 cm and the radius of the bigger circle is 4 cm. The area between the inner and the outer circles is shaded.

hints:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{25\pi\,\text{cm}^2} - \greenD{9\pi\,\text{cm}^2} \\\\
&= 16\pi\,\text{cm}^2 \\\\
&\approx 50.27\,\text{cm}^2
\end{align}$

options.alt: A circle with a smaller circle inside it. The circles share a common center. The radius of the smaller circle is 3 cm and the radius of the bigger circle is 5 cm. The region between both the circles is shaded.

hints:

assessment_items.question_content:
A circle with radius of $\greenD{2\,\text{cm}}$ sits inside a $\blueD{11\,\text{cm} \times 12\,\text{cm}}$ rectangle. 

** What is the area of the shaded region? **  
*Round your final answer to the nearest hundredth.* 

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
First, calculate the area of the whole figure, including the unshaded area.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a rectangle is the length times the width.
 
$\blueD{11\,\text{cm} \times 12\,\text{cm} = 132\,\text{cm}^2}$

Next, calculate the area of the inner figure.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a circle is $\pi r^2$.

$\greenD{\pi \times 2\,\text{cm} \times 2 \,\text{cm}= 4\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$
Finally, subtract the area of the inner circle from the area of the outer rectangle.
$\blueD{132\,\text{cm}^2} - \greenD{4\pi\,\text{cm}^2}\approx 119.43\,\text{cm}^2$

options.alt: A rectangle with a circle inside it. One side of the rectangle is labelled as 12 cm. The adjacent side is labelled as 11 cm. The radius of the circle is 2 cm. The region between the circle and the rectangle is shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:

*Round your final answer to the nearest hundredth.*

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
First, calculate the area of the whole figure, including the unshaded area.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a circle is $\pi r^2$.

$\blueD{\pi \times 12\,\text{cm} \times 12 \,\text{cm}= 144\pi\,\text{cm}^2}$

Next, calculate the area of the inner figure.

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of a rectangle is the length times the width.
 
$\greenD{5\,\text{cm} \times 11\,\text{cm} = 55\,\text{cm}^2}$
Finally, subtract the area of the inner rectangle from the area of the outer circle.
$\blueD{144\pi\,\text{cm}^2} - \greenD{55\,\text{cm}^2}\approx 397.39\,\text{cm}^2$

Finally, subtract the area of the inner rectangle from the area of the outer circle.

$\greenD{12\,\text{cm} \times 2\,\text{cm} = 24\,\text{cm}^2}$
